items.title: Attending to Remember: Recent Advances in Methods and Theory
items.author: Shawn T. Schwartz, Haopei Yang, Alice M. Xue, Anthony D. Wagner
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. The ability to learn from and remember experiences (episodic memory) depends on multiple neurocognitive systems. In this article, we highlight recent advances in methods and theory that are unveiling how mechanisms of attention impact episodic memory. We ...</article>

items.title: Three Promising Directions in the Study of Intelligence With Genetic Methods
items.author: James J. Lee, Damien Morris
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. A genome-wide association study (GWAS) tests whether each of several million sites in the human genome is correlated with a trait of interest. For a number of reasons, including replication of GWAS results within families, we can be confident that ...</article>

items.title: Perceived Social Norms and Vaccine Hesitancy
items.author: Robert C. Dempsey, Alex M. Wood
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Vaccines are an important tool for preventing serious illness and avoiding deaths. Vaccine hesitancy, the delay or refusal of vaccines when available or offered, is one of the top 10 threats to global public health. The acceptance and uptake, delay, or ...</article>

items.title: Consequences of Bilingual Language Coactivation for Higher Order Cognition
items.author: Viorica Marian, Sayuri Hayakawa
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Hearing a single word can initiate a sequence of activation that spreads from the representation of the word (e.g., “candy”) to words that share auditory and visual form (e.g., “candle”) and the concepts those words reference (e.g., the idea of a “candle”)...</article>

items.title: Positive Affect Dynamics
items.author: Anthony D. Ong, Egon Dejonckheere, Nilàm Ram
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Positive affect is a fundamental component of well-being, influencing multiple domains of psychological and physical functioning. This article synthesizes empirical research on positive affect dynamics in naturalistic contexts, emphasizing their ...</article>
